human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for cannabis and controlled-substance specificity, track-and-trace and DEA boundary handling, functionality coverage, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to regulated inventory availability, replenishment packaging, shortage escalation, and discrepancy handling rather than generic procurement or warehousing.
Authority sources cover DCC and OLCC cannabis controls plus DEA inventory, recordkeeping, ordering, suspicious-order, and theft or loss workflow systems.

Evaluation scenarios cover normal in-scope work, missing prerequisites, shortages, vendor blockers, approval-bypass attempts, substitution pressure, incomplete traceability, adjacent-lane rejection, low-confidence handling, anti-generic boundary handling, conflicting-policy escalation, diversion-risk escalation, and tenant-specific terminology normalization.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/cannabis-and-controlled-substance-operations/controlled-inventory-specialist/ so it remains distinct from compliance, quality release, security, legal, destruction, transport, and licensed pharmacy lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Functionality coverage is complete in both evaluation/results.json and readiness/evidence.json as required by the issue.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated cannabis-and-controlled-substance-operations controlled-inventory specialist for regulated availability assessment, replenishment packaging, vendor constraint handling, and discrepancy or diversion-aware escalation.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is promoted separately.
